title: Cumulative global production of plastics
subtitle: Cumulative annual production of polymer resin and fibers.
caption:
Cumulative global production of plastics

Cumulative annual production of polymer resin and fibers.

The area chart displays the cumulative global production of plastics from 1950 to 2019, measured in billion tonnes. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 10 billion tonnes, while the horizontal axis marks decades from 1950 to 2010, ending at 2019. The data series begins at 0 billion tonnes in 1950 and remains visually flat near the baseline until approximately 1960. From 1960 to 1980, the shaded area rises gradually, reaching a value below 1 billion tonnes by 1980. The curve steepens noticeably between 1980 and 2000, crossing the 2 billion tonne mark around 1990 and approaching 4 billion tonnes by the year 2000. Growth accelerates further in the 21st century; the line passes 6 billion tonnes around 2010 and continues to climb sharply. By the final data point in 2019, the cumulative total reaches approximately 9.5 billion tonnes. The visual trajectory forms a distinct exponential curve, with the rate of accumulation increasing significantly in each successive decade, particularly after 1990. The steepest section of the curve occurs between 2000 and 2019, where the cumulative total more than doubles from roughly 4 billion to nearly 10 billion tonnes.
